# Research: bucket-pick-format-standards

*Auto-generated by AIDLC planning session*

**Question:** What are the common bucket-pick and pick-X-of-Y pool formats used in real golf country clubs? Are there standard configurations the system should support out of the box?

---

# Bucket-Pick & Pick-X-of-Y Golf Pool Format Standards

## Overview

Golf pools at country clubs and among recreational groups typically fall into two structural families: **bucket/tier picks** and **flat pick-X-of-Y**. Both are used for major tournaments (Masters, US Open, The Open Championship, PGA Championship, Players Championship) and occasionally for Ryder Cup or Presidents Cup team formats.

---

## 1. Flat Pick-X-of-Y Format

### How It Works
Each participant selects exactly **X golfers** from the full field of **Y** available players. Scoring is based on the cumulative performance of those picks over the tournament.

### Common Configurations

| Format | Picks | Field Size | Notes |
|--------|-------|------------|-------|
| Pick 5 of 50 | 5 | 50 | Small club pools, casual |
| Pick 6 of Field | 6 | Full field (~80–156) | Most common Masters format |
| Pick 8 of Field | 8 | Full field | PGA Championship / US Open (larger field) |
| Pick 10 of Field | 10 | Full field | Used when scoring = best N of X picks |
| Pick 4, score best 2 | 4 | Full field | "Safety net" variant |
| Pick 6, score best 4 | 6 | Full field | Very common club variant |

### Scoring Variants
- **Sum all picks**: Total strokes/score from all selected golfers
- **Best N of X**: Only the top N performers among your picks count (discards worst)
- **Tournament position points**: Points awarded by finishing position (1st=100pts, 2nd=75pts, etc.) summed across picks
- **Cut survival bonus**: Extra points if a picked golfer makes the cut

---

## 2. Bucket / Tier Pick Format

### How It Works
The field is divided into **tiers (buckets)** of golfers ranked by world ranking, odds, or perceived skill. Each participant must select **one (or more) golfer(s) from each bucket**. This forces differentiation and prevents everyone from simply picking the top-ranked players.

### Standard Bucket Structures

#### Classic 5-Bucket Format (Masters — 50-man field variant)
| Bucket | Rank Range | Examples (illustrative) | Picks Required |
|--------|-----------|--------------------------|---------------|
| A (Elite) | World #1–10 | Top favorites, past champions | Pick 1 |
| B (Contenders) | World #11–25 | Consistent top-10 players | Pick 1 |
| C (Sleepers) | World #26–50 | In-form players, course fits | Pick 1 |
| D (Long shots) | World #51–100 | Occasional winners, value picks | Pick 1 |
| E (Field players) | World #101+ | Dark horses, local favorites | Pick 1 |

**Total: 5 picks, one per bucket**

#### 4-Bucket Format (Most Common Club Standard)
| Bucket | Tier Description | Typical Size | Picks Required |
|--------|-----------------|--------------|---------------|
| 1 | Top favorites (oddsmakers' top ~8) | 8 players | Pick 1 |
| 2 | Mid-tier contenders | 12–15 players | Pick 1 |
| 3 | Sleepers / value | 15–20 players | Pick 1 |
| 4 | Field / long shots | Remainder | Pick 1 or 2 |

**Total: 4–5 picks**

#### 3-Bucket "Simple" Format
| Bucket | Description | Size | Picks |
|--------|-------------|------|-------|
| Stars | Top 10 favorites | 10 | Pick 2 |
| Contenders | 11–30 ranked | 20 | Pick 2 |
| Field | 31+ | Remainder | Pick 2 |

**Total: 6 picks, 2 per bucket**

#### 6-Bucket "Deep Dive" Format (larger pools, 30+ participants)
| Bucket | Label | Basis | Picks |
|--------|-------|-------|-------|
| 1 | Tier 1 | World #1–5 | Pick 1 |
| 2 | Tier 2 | World #6–15 | Pick 1 |
| 3 | Tier 3 | World #16–30 | Pick 1 |
| 4 | Tier 4 | World #31–50 | Pick 1 |
| 5 | Tier 5 | World #51–80 | Pick 1 |
| 6 | Tier 6 | World #81+ | Pick 1 |

**Total: 6 picks, strict one-per-tier**

---

## 3. Hybrid Formats

### Anchor + Bucket
- **1 "anchor" pick** from any tier (free choice, no restriction)
- **3–4 bucket picks** from assigned tiers
- Anchor pick counts double or at 1.5x in scoring

### Captain's Pick
- One pick is designated "captain" — counts 2x or 1.5x
- Remaining picks are standard bucket or flat picks
- Common in fantasy-golf style pools wanting strategic depth

### Snake Draft (informal club pools)
- Pool participants draft in snake order (1,2,3...N, N...3,2,1)
- No buckets; draft order creates natural tier differentiation
- More common in season-long pools than single-event

---

## 4. Bucket Definition Methods

How clubs define bucket membership varies:

| Method | Description | Pros | Cons |
|--------|-------------|------|------|
| **World Golf Ranking** | Official OWGR at time of field announcement | Objective, easy to verify | Can misrepresent course-fit or current form |
| **Betting Odds** | Pre-tournament odds (e.g., top 8 at <15:1) | Reflects market consensus on winning probability | Odds shift; need to snapshot at a fixed time |
| **Manual Admin Assignment** | Pool admin places players into buckets by judgment | Maximum flexibility | Subjective, potential for complaints |
| **Even Split** | Alphabetical or random within ranked order | Simple, reproducible | Ignores skill differentiation |
| **Points/FedEx/OWGR Hybrid** | Weighted composite ranking | Nuanced | Complex to explain and maintain |

**Most common in practice**: Manual admin assignment using OWGR + current-season form as a guide, snapshotted at the Monday before the tournament.

---

## 5. Scoring Systems

### Stroke-Based (most common for majors)
- Pick cumulative score relative to par across all 4 rounds
- WD/DQ/MC players score a fixed penalty (e.g., +10 per missed round, or last-place score)
- **Lowest total wins** (or lowest average, same result)

### Points-Based
| Finish | Points |
|--------|--------|
| 1st | 100 |
| 2nd | 75 |
| 3rd | 60 |
| 4th | 50 |
| 5th | 45 |
| 6–10 | 35 |
| 11–20 | 20 |
| Made cut | 5 |
| Missed cut | 0 |
| WD/DQ | -5 |

Summed across all picks. **Highest total wins.**

### Position-Change Bonus (rarer, more engagement-driving)
- Points for each position gained per round (Eagles, birdies on leaderboard position)
- Rewards picks who charge up the board

---

## 6. Tiebreaker Standards

Most clubs use one or more of these, in order:

1. **Tiebreaker pick**: Before the tournament, each participant picks a golfer's exact 72-hole score (or final score to par). Closest wins.
2. **Winning score guess**: Predict the winning total score; closest breaks tie.
3. **Number of birdies by picks**: Cumulative birdies made by all selected golfers.
4. **Head-to-head**: Both participants re-rank their picks; higher-ranked pick's performance wins.
5. **Split the pot**: Prize money divided equally among tied participants.

---

## 7. Standard Rules & Edge Cases

### Cut Rule
- Golfers who miss the cut are typically penalized rather than zeroed out
- Common penalties:
  - **Fixed penalty**: +8 to +12 strokes for each missed round (rounds 3 & 4)
  - **Last place score**: MC'd golfer assigned the worst score among those who made cut
  - **Zero/DNP**: MC = 0 points in points-based systems

### Withdrawal (WD) / Disqualification (DQ)
- WD before round 1: Usually allowed to replace pick (if pool format allows a "free swap")
- WD during round 1: Scored on completed holes only + penalty for incomplete rounds
- Most club pools: WD after round 1 tee-off = treated same as MC

### Replacement Pick Window
- Some formats allow 1 free replacement if a WD/injury occurs before the player tees off in round 1
- Replacement must come from the same bucket (bucket format) or is unrestricted (flat format)
- Deadline: typically 15 minutes before the first tee time of round 1

### Duplicate Picks
- In pools with many participants, the same golfer can often be picked by multiple people
- Some pools enforce **unique picks** (each golfer can only be selected by one participant) — more of a draft format
- Bucket formats almost never enforce uniqueness

---

## 8. Recommended Out-of-Box Configurations

Based on real-world club usage patterns, these are the configurations worth supporting as templates:

### Config 1: "Classic Club Masters Pool" (most popular)
- **Type**: 4-bucket
- **Picks**: 1 per bucket (4 total)
- **Scoring**: Stroke-based, sum of all picks
- **MC Penalty**: Last-place score for each missed round
- **Tiebreaker**: Predict winning score to par

### Config 2: "Pick 6, Best 4 Count"
- **Type**: Flat pick-6
- **Picks**: 6 from full field
- **Scoring**: Stroke-based, best 4 of 6 picks count
- **MC Penalty**: +10 per missed round
- **Tiebreaker**: Closest-to-the-pin style winning score guess

### Config 3: "5-Tier Deep Draft"
- **Type**: 5-bucket
- **Picks**: 1 per bucket (5 total)
- **Scoring**: Points-based by finish position
- **MC**: 0 points
- **Tiebreaker**: Split pot

### Config 4: "Captain's Choice"
- **Type**: Flat pick-5 with captain designation
- **Picks**: 5 from full field; designate 1 as captain (2x points)
- **Scoring**: Points-based
- **MC**: 0 points
- **Tiebreaker**: Predict number of eagles in tournament

### Config 5: "Simple 3-Bucket Casual"
- **Type**: 3-bucket
- **Picks**: 2 per bucket (6 total)
- **Scoring**: Stroke-based, sum all 6
- **MC Penalty**: +8 per missed round
- **Tiebreaker**: First tiebreaker pick (bonus golfer named at submission)

---

## 9. Pool Size Recommendations by Format

| Pool Size | Recommended Format |
|-----------|-------------------|
| 5–10 participants | Flat Pick-6 or Simple 3-Bucket |
| 10–20 participants | 4-Bucket Classic or Pick-6 Best-4 |
| 20–40 participants | 5-Bucket or Captain's Choice |
| 40+ participants | 6-Bucket or Snake Draft |

---

## 10. Implementation Considerations

- **Bucket assignment snapshot date**: Store which OWGR/odds were used and when; disputes arise if this isn't fixed
- **Field finalization**: Bucket assignments should lock when the official field is announced (typically Tuesday before the tournament)
- **Pick deadline**: Industry standard is the first tee time of round 1; some pools use 24 hours before
- **Scoring update frequency**: Most clubs are fine with once-per-round updates; real-time is a nice-to-have
- **Public vs. private leaderboard**: Some participants prefer others can't see picks until the tournament starts (prevents copycat picking)
- **Prize structure**: Typically winner-take-all or 60/30/10 split for top 3; needs to be configurable
